title: Pollo alla Cacciatora Hunter Style Chicken

seo.title: Authentic Chicken Cacciatore Recipe (Pollo alla Cacciatora)
seo.metaDescription: Master authentic Italian Chicken Cacciatore (Pollo alla Cacciatora) with our easy, flavorful recipe. Tender chicken, rich tomato sauce, and fresh herbs. Perfect for dinner tonight!
seo.h1: Authentic Italian Chicken Cacciatore (Pollo alla Cacciatora)
h2Headings: Ingredients for Authentic Pollo alla Cacciatora
How to Make Hunter Style Chicken (Step-by-Step)
Pro Tips for the Best Chicken Cacciatore
Serving Suggestions for Pollo alla Cacciatora
seo.imageAlt: Authentic Italian Chicken Cacciatore (Pollo alla Cacciatora) with tender chicken, bell peppers, and rich tomato sauce.
seo.imageCaption: Enjoy the classic flavors of Italy with our homemade Chicken Cacciatore recipe, perfect for a hearty family dinner.

description: Pollo alla Cacciatora is a rustic Italian classic, featuring tender chicken braised in a rich, savory tomato sauce with bell peppers, onions, and aromatic herbs. This 'hunter style' dish brings the hearty flavors of the Italian countryside directly to your table, perfect for a comforting family meal. It's a symphony of textures and tastes, simple yet profoundly satisfying.

instructions.title: Prepare and Season the Chicken
instructions.text: Pat the chicken thighs dry with paper towels. Season generously all over with salt and freshly ground black pepper. This step is crucial for developing a flavorful crust.

instructions.title: Brown the Chicken
instructions.text: Heat the olive oil in a large heavy-bottomed pot or Dutch oven over medium-high heat. Once hot, add the chicken thighs skin-side down and cook for 5-7 minutes until the skin is deeply golden brown and crispy. Flip and cook for another 3-4 minutes on the other side. Remove chicken from the pot and set aside.

instructions.title: Sauté the Vegetables
instructions.text: Reduce heat to medium. Add the chopped onion and bell peppers to the pot, scraping up any browned bits from the chicken. Sauté for 5-7 minutes until the vegetables start to soften. Add the minced garlic and cook for another minute until fragrant.

instructions.title: Deglaze and Add Tomatoes
instructions.text: Pour in the white wine, stirring and scraping the bottom of the pot to deglaze and release all the flavorful fond. Let the wine simmer for 2-3 minutes until it has reduced by half. Stir in the crushed tomatoes and chicken broth.

instructions.title: Simmer with Herbs
instructions.text: Return the browned chicken to the pot, nestling it into the sauce. Add the fresh rosemary sprigs, thyme sprigs, and bay leaf. Bring the sauce to a gentle simmer, then reduce the heat to low, cover the pot, and cook for 25-30 minutes, or until the chicken is tender and cooked through.

instructions.title: Finish and Serve
instructions.text: Remove the lid, stir in the pitted Kalamata olives, and let it simmer uncovered for an additional 5 minutes to allow the sauce to thicken slightly. Remove the herb sprigs and bay leaf. Taste and adjust seasoning with salt and pepper if needed. Garnish with fresh chopped parsley before serving.

tips: For the best flavor, always brown your chicken thoroughly before simmering. This creates a rich base for your sauce.
If you dont have white wine, you can substitute with extra chicken broth and a splash of red wine vinegar for acidity.
Pollo alla Cacciatora pairs wonderfully with creamy polenta, crusty Italian bread, or a simple pasta like pappardelle to soak up all the delicious sauce.
article: Pollo alla Cacciatora Hunter Style Chicken is a beloved Italian Chicken Recipes dish enjoyed across Italian. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
